fear: That the coded letter he passed to the customs agent will be found and traced back to him — it bears a cipher only he uses in his account ledgers.
name: Thomas Greenleaf
role: Sons of Liberty treasurer and merchant clerk
voice: Soft-spoken and precise, favoring careful qualifications — 'I believe,' 'as I understand it,' 'one ought to be careful.' He never raises his voice and smiles just a moment too late.
secrets: He received thirty pounds sterling from the East India Company's agent in exchange for the boarding plan.
The coded letter discovered at the Counting House was written in his hand, using a cipher from his personal ledger.
knowledge: He knows the exact time and method the boarding party plans to use to board the Dartmouth.
He knows the name of his customs contact, Agent Silas Dawes, and the location of their last meeting at the Counting House.
He is aware that Samuel Adams suspects a leak but believes the suspicion currently falls on Reverend Willard.
publicFace: A mild-mannered, bookish man who handles the group's accounts with quiet efficiency — trusted by nearly everyone for his discretion and loyalty to the cause.
represents: REQUIRES FACT-CHECK — verify whether this is a documented historical individual from colonial Boston, 1773-1775
trustLogic: He opens up when he believes the player is focused on Reverend Willard as a suspect — he will even volunteer mildly incriminating details about the reverend to redirect attention. He shuts down immediately if the player mentions the Counting House or the name Dawes.
introAnchor: the Sons of Liberty treasurer, a mild-mannered bookish man trusted by nearly everyone for his discretion — which is exactly what makes him dangerous
privateGoal: Greenleaf has been secretly paid by the East India Company's Boston agent to pass intelligence about Patriot operations; he hopes to collect his fee and quietly leave Boston before anyone suspects him.

aggressionProfile.fleeStyle: He moves with surprising speed for a bookish man, ducking into the alley behind the Green Dragon and heading toward the waterfront.
aggressionProfile.chaseStyle: He shouts that he is being robbed to draw attention from bystanders, hoping the commotion slows pursuit.
aggressionProfile.mildPressure: He grows very still, adjusts his spectacles, and deflects with bureaucratic detail — asking the player to clarify exactly what they mean, buying time to compose himself.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will never voluntarily admit he wrote the coded letter — he insists it is a forgery even when confronted with it directly.
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: If the player presents both the coded letter and the customs receipt simultaneously, Greenleaf bolts for the door.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: He becomes indignant, invoking his years of service to the Sons of Liberty and suggesting the player has been manipulated by enemies of the cause.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: When cornered, he collapses into tearful confession, begging for mercy and claiming he was coerced by threats to his family — a partial truth designed to win sympathy.
